calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Determining Title To Property
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Alexander Hoffman.

The Court stated that it seems like the property has already been characterized, questions why they are doing an 850 petition when Mr. Hoffman has represented to the Court under penalty of perjury that these are estate assets and stated that Mr. Hoffman marshalled the cash under Special Administration into an estate account. Mr. Siracusa stated that he disagrees with that, made reference to Heggstad, stated that the focus should be on the intent of the Settlor, that clearly the Settlor intended to transfer the assets and that the bank accounts were listed on the schedule. Mr. Siracusa also made reference to Ukkestad, stated that there is sufficient evidence to demonstrate the intent of 850, and that Mr. Hoffman had to file a petition for special administration because the real property is in the trust and he needed access to the funds in those bank accounts to pay the mortgage and keep the property out of foreclosure. The Court noted that there was a way for Mr. Hoffman to deal with this. Mr. Siracusa stated that there was potential litigation against another Attorney that was involved with the Grandfather and that they probably would have gotten objections to an 850 which is why they did not file it right away. The Court noted that Mr. Siracusa's Client was wearing the hat of special administration swearing under penalty and perjury that those were estate assets, and that he got the benefit of that and moved funds. Mr. Siracusa stated that his Client filed the 850 and then withdrew the petition for probate, that he would like to further brief this, that the fact that the accounts are sitting in special administration accounts he believes the Trustee could still pursue an 850 petition and get the accounts transferred from the estate into the trust and that he and his Client were not hiding anything from the Court. Mr. Siracusa also stated that he remembers talking with a Probate Examiner about Heggstad, that the reality is that these assets were sitting in the Decedent's name so technically are in the estate until there is a court order to transfer, that he does not feel his Client about anything by listing the character of the assets, that the Court has broad and equitable discretion and that having to do probate would be a hardship on his Client. The Court expressed concern about the same person making different representations to the Court. Mr. Siracusa stated that the trust was attached to the petition, that he can file a declaration from his Client, that there was no intent to mislead the Court, that there was more than one late mortgage payment (3 months behind) and that there is complication because they don't even have probate open. The Petition for Order Determining Title to Property is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Title To Property
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Continued To Jul-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
